{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 =
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Saeki clan|ja|佐伯氏|lt=Saeki-no-Atai|lt_ja=佐伯直}}, {{ill|Harima clan|ja|播磨氏|lt=Harima-no-Atai|lt_ja=播磨直}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Ikojiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|伊許自別命}} (great-grandson of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}})
|種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}}
|出身地 =
|根拠地 = Harima Province<br />(later {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 =
}}
'''Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''harima no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''harima kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled [[#Territory|Harima Province]] (western {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', they were descendants of {{ill|Inase-iribiko-no-Miko|ja|稲背入彦皇子}}, an imperial prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, that prince's son '''Mimorowake-no-Mikoto''' was enfeoffed in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}}, and from then on the clan name became "Harima-wake." When {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} made a {{ill|royal progress|ja|行幸}} to Harima Province, Mimorowake-no-Mikoto's son '''{{ill|Ikojiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|伊許自別命}}''' (Aratsu-no-Mikoto), the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko, was made ''{{ill|tomo no miyatsuko|ja|伴造}}'' and given charge of the Saeki-be of the province, and his surname was changed to "Harima-wake-Saeki-no-Atai." In the creation of the {{ill|Kōgo-nen-jaku|ja|庚午年籍}} register in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} ([[670]]), the three characters "Harima-wake" were dropped and they came to be called "Saeki-no-Atai"{{Refnest|''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', {{ill|Ukyō|ja|右京}} Kōbetsu (lower){{Sfn|内外書籍株式会社|1932|pp=448-449}}. Also {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}} Kōbetsu section{{Sfn|内外書籍株式会社|1932|p=457}}.}}.

Regarding {{ill|Inase-iribiko-no-Miko|ja|稲背入彦皇子}}, the ''Nihon Shoki'', vol. 7, Emperor Keikō, Year 4, 2nd month, ''kasshi'' day entry calls him "the founding ancestor of the Harima-wake," and the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', vol. 7, Tennō Hongi, closing section of Emperor Keikō's chapter, calls him "ancestor of the Harima-wake."

=== Genealogy Based on Historical Records and Traditions ===

<div class="NavFrame" style="width:100%;">
 <div class="NavHead" style="padding:1.5px; line-height:1.7; letter-spacing:1px;">House of the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko</div>
 <div class="NavContent" style="text-align:left;">
;Bold = head of clan; solid line = biological child; dotted line = adopted.<!--構成の都合で出生順より組み替え-->
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:85%}}
{{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'''}}
{{familytree |border=0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Inase-iribiko-no-Miko|ja|稲背入彦皇子}} (Harima-wake-no-Mikoto?)'''}}
{{familytree |border=0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Mimorowake-no-Mikoto|ja|御諸別命}} (Asotake-no-Mikoto?)'''}}
{{familytree |border=0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree |border=0|||||||01||||| 01='''{{ill|Ikojiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|伊許自別命}} (Aratsu-no-Mikoto)'''}}

{{familytree/end}}
</div>
</div>

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Saeki clan|ja|佐伯直|lt=Saeki clan|lt_ja=佐伯氏}}''' (''saeki-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai|ja|直}}). Also '''{{ill|Harima clan|ja|播磨氏}}''' (''harima-uji''; kabane of {{ill|Atai|ja|直}}, later {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}).

== History ==
In the ''{{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|ja|播磨国風土記}}'', '''{{ill|Saeki-no-Agano-ko|ja|佐伯阿俄能胡|lt=Agano-ko|lt_ja=阿我乃古}}''' (''agano-ko'') is said to have been granted the title of Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=福島好和 |date=1995 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10236/4985 |title=播磨国の佐伯直氏について |journal=人文論究 |volume=45 |issue=1 |pages=1-15 |ISSN=02866773 |hdl=10236/4985}}</ref>.

According to the {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} section of the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', the court lady Kuwata-no-Kugahime was given to '''Hayamachi''' (''hayamachi''), the ancestor of the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

According to the {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} section of the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', in Year 40 of Emperor Nintoku, {{ill|Saeki-no-Agano-ko|ja|佐伯阿俄能胡|lt=Agano-ko|lt_ja=阿俄能胡}} pursued {{ill|Prince Hayabusa-wake|ja|隼別皇子}} (''hayabusa-wake no miko'') and {{ill|Princess Metori|ja|雌鳥皇女}} (''metori no hime-miko'') along with {{ill|Kibi-no-Homuchibe-no-Ofuna|ja|吉備品遅部雄鯽}} (''kibi no homuchibe no ofuna'') by imperial order, captured them at Komoshironono in {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}, and killed them on the spot. At that time, contrary to orders, he and Ofuna stole a jewel that Princess Metori had been wearing.

Later, in the 11th month of the same year when the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} was held, there was a {{ill|Toyo-no-Akari-no-Sechi-e|ja|豊明節会}}, and at the drinking feast this was exposed. Agano-ko confessed and offered up land to escape a death sentence. That land came to be called Tamate (there is a Tamate Hill in {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡}} of Yamato Province, corresponding to present-day {{ill|Gose, Nara|ja|御所市|lt=Gose|lt_ja=御所市}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}).

== People ==
*'''{{ill|Inase-iribiko-no-Miko|ja|稲背入彦皇子}}''' (''inase-iribiko-no-miko'')
*:Imperial prince of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
*'''Mimorowake-no-Mikoto''' (''mimorowake-no-mikoto'')
*:Son of Inase-iribiko-no-Miko, he was enfeoffed in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}} and took the clan name "Harima-wake."
*'''{{ill|Iwasakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|石坂比売命}}''' (''iwasakahime-no-mikoto'')
*:According to the ''Harima no Kuni Fudoki'', when {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} was headed for the {{ill|Subjugation of the Three Kingdoms of Korea|ja|三韓征伐}} and prayed to the gods, {{ill|Nihotsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|爾保都比売命}} possessed Iwasakahime-no-Mikoto of the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko and said, "If you enshrine me, you will be able to subdue {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} at {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹波}}," giving her red earth. When applied to the "heavenly reverse-halberd," divine ship, and war dress, nothing could obstruct her, it is said. Because the original text refers only to "the kuni no miyatsuko," there is also a theory that she was a figure of the {{ill|Akashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|明石国造}}.
*'''{{ill|Ikojiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|伊許自別命}}''' (''ikojiwake-no-mikoto'', also Aratsu-no-Mikoto)
*:Son of Mimorowake-no-Mikoto. When {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} made a {{ill|royal progress|ja|行幸}} to Harima Province, he was made ''{{ill|tomo no miyatsuko|ja|伴造}}'' and given charge of the Saeki-be of the province, and his surname was changed to "Harima-wake-Saeki-no-Atai."
*'''{{ill|Saeki-no-Agano-ko|ja|佐伯阿俄能胡|lt=Agano-ko|lt_ja=阿我乃古}}''' (''agano-ko'')
*:Kuni no miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|lt=Emperor Ōjin|lt_ja=応神朝}}. Said to have been granted the position of kuni no miyatsuko by {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. He pursued and subdued {{ill|Prince Hayabusa-wake|ja|隼別皇子}} and {{ill|Princess Metori|ja|雌鳥皇女}}, who had led a rebellion.
*'''{{ill|Toyoshiwake-no-Mikoto|ja|豊忍別命}}''' (''toyoshiwake-no-mikoto'')
*:According to the Ansō-ri entry in the {{ill|Shikama District|ja|飾磨郡|lt=Shikama District|lt_ja=餝磨郡}} section of the ''{{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|ja|播磨国風土記}}'', when {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} was touring {{ill|Tajima Province|ja|但馬国|lt=Tajima|lt_ja=但遅麻}}, his crown fell off during the journey, for which Toyoshiwake-no-Mikoto, the kuni no miyatsuko, was stripped of his name. Thereupon, {{ill|Akone-no-Mikoto|ja|阿胡尼命}}, the {{ill|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}, apologized to the emperor and offered up 40 ''chōbu'' of rice-fields in place of salt, by which he was forgiven<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=神崎勝 |date=2012-03 |url=https://doi.org/10.34382/00006347 |title=国造とそのクニについて(再論) : 篠川賢氏のご批判にお答えする |journal=立命館文学= 立命館文学 |ISSN=0287-7015 |publisher=立命館大学人文学会 |volume=626 |pages=1225-1242 |doi=10.34382/00006347 |hdl=10367/4799}}</ref>. The original text records Toyoshiwake-no-Mikoto only as "kuni no miyatsuko," and there is a theory that he was the {{ill|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}.
*'''{{ill|Hayamachi|ja|速待}}''' (''hayamachi'')
*:Kuni no miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|lt=Emperor Nintoku|lt_ja=仁徳朝}}. The court lady Kuwata-no-Kugahime is said to have been given to '''Hayamachi''' (''hayamachi''), the ancestor of the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

== Descendants ==
*According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Emperor {{ill|Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|lt=Kinmei|lt_ja=欽明}} 17 ([[556]]), 1st month entry, Abe-no-Omi, Saeki-no-Muraji, and '''Harima-no-Atai (name blank)''' led the naval forces of Tsukushi Province to deliver the {{ill|King of Baekje|ja|百済王}}.
*In the "{{ill|Shōsōin Documents|ja|正倉院文書}}," a figure thought to be a district official of {{ill|Shikama District|ja|飾磨郡}} appears as Daryō of outer Junior 8th Rank Upper — '''Harima (name blank)'''.
*In a document dated {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 5 ([[721]]), 9th month, 11th day, the name of Lady-in-waiting Junior 5th Rank Lower '''Harima-no-Atai Tsukitari''' appears.
*In the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' article of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 2 ([[730]]), 3rd month, 27th day, the name '''{{ill|Harima-no-Otoyasu|ja|播磨乙安|lt=Harima-no-Atai Otoyasu|lt_ja=播磨直乙安}}''' appears.
*In the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' entry for the 1st month of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応}} 1 ([[781]]), among those granted outer Junior 5th Rank Lower was Junior Initial Rank Lower '''Saeki-no-Atai Morishige''', a man of {{ill|Ibo District|ja|揖保郡}} in Harima Province.
*In a document dated {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 12 ([[793]]), 5th month, 14th day, one can confirm Acting Shushu Senior 8th Rank Upper '''Harima-no-Atai (name blank)'''.
*According to the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}}'' entry for the 3rd month of {{ill|Jōwa (Japan)|ja|承和 (日本)|lt=Jōwa|lt_ja=承和}} 13 ([[864]]), Senior 8th Rank Upper Sani '''Saeki-no-Atai Yakamori''' and Junior Initial Rank Lower '''Saeki-no-Atai Nakanari''', both from Ibo District, moved from Ibo District to Ukyō 6-jō 2-bō.
*In a document dated {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 5 ([[942]]), 11th month, 24th day, one can confirm Shōsō '''Harima-no-Fuminaka'''.
*In a document dated {{ill|Chōtoku|ja|長徳}} 2 ([[996]]), 1st month, 25th day, the names of Harima Ōsakan Senior 6th Rank Upper '''Harima-no-Sukune Toyonari''' and Harima Jō Senior 6th Rank Upper '''Harima-no-Sukune Morinobu''' can be confirmed.
*In a Harima provincial ordinance dated {{ill|Chōwa|ja|長和}} 4 ([[1015]]), 10th month, 15th day, the names Sō-kenkō-no-Jō '''Harima-no-Denya''', Kenkō Uchidoneri '''Harima-no-Kana''', and Bettō '''Harima-no-Okimasa''' appear, along with the sealed {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|lt=Harima Ōsakan|lt_ja=播磨大掾}} '''Harima-no-Sukune (name blank)'''.

=== ''Kitadera Chishiki-kyō'' ===
In the ''Kitadera Chishiki-kyō'' — in which the congregations of temples including Kitadera in {{ill|Kamo District|ja|賀茂郡}} of {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} gathered in {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 6 ([[734]]) to copy the "Daichido-kyō" — there appear figures of the Saeki-no-Atai, Harima-no-Atai, and Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko clans, all descendants of the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|author=松本信道 |date=1979-03 |url=http://repo.komazawa-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/all/20439/ |title=奈良時代の優婆塞・優婆夷に関する一考察 ‐特に知識集団との関連について‐ |journal=駒澤史学 |ISSN=04506928 |publisher=駒澤大学文学部史学会 |volume=26 |pages=24 |naid=110007002867}}</ref>.
*Saeki-no-Atai Ayako Ubai
*Saeki-no-Atai Tomime
*Saeki-no-Atai Azumahito
*Saeki-no-Atai Maro
*Saeki-no-Atai Hayashi
*Harima-no-Atai Hōmaro
*Harima-no-Atai Tarihito
*Harima-no-Atai Funawa-hō
*Harima-no-Atai Meime
*Harima-no-Atai Nachakime
*Harima-no-Atai Akamaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Kakuhashi
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Komaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Otomaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Shimakuni
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Toshisumaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Moroto
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Yamamori
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Kunimori
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Wakatoji
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Hiroyama
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Okimi
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Aragumi
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Himyō
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Okimi
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Ōnari
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Dai-zen
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Tojiko
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Kotoji
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Kotama
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Etamaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Takatako
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Akatoji
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Chikuni
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Tsunomaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Chimaro
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Seisū
*Harima-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Hiromaro

== Shrine ==
At {{ill|Shirakuni Shrine|ja|白国神社}} in {{ill|Himeji, Hyōgo|ja|姫路市|lt=Himeji|lt_ja=姫路市}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, Inase-iribiko-no-Mikoto, Asotake-no-Mikoto, and Aratsu-no-Mikoto are enshrined. It is said to have been enshrined by Saeki-no-Sukune Geiko-ta, the 21st-generation descendant of the Saeki-no-Atai clan, in the 3rd month of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 2 ([[730]]).

== Tomb ==
There is a theory that the {{ill|Danjōzan Kofun|ja|壇場山古墳}}, the largest kofun in {{ill|Himeji, Hyōgo|ja|姫路市|lt=Himeji City|lt_ja=姫路市}}, is the tomb of the Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko<ref>Himeji City Board of Education (ed.), "{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/kanko/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2172/1.pdf Visiting Himeji, Land of the Fudoki]}}"</ref>.

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:はりまのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:播磨国の氏族]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:針間国造]]
